catalysts_general: Inspire Medical sleep apnea implant; FDA-approved therapy with growing physician adoption; GLP-1 weight loss drugs perceived as competitive threat (Tier 3: analyst reports)

Discovery: Sleep apnea medtech at fresh 52-week low of ~$51, down over 65% from highs; fast-growing and largely untapped OSA neurostimulation market; just upgraded to Strong-Buy by Zacks (Mar 25); catalyst: CMS introduced dedicated C-codes for Inspire V system, removing hospital billing hurdles and clearing path for accelerated adoption; analyst consensus target $104 (~100% upside); bear case is competition from GLP-1 drugs reducing sleep apnea prevalence
value_picks.analyst_take: Sleep apnea device leader at 52% drawdown; FDA-approved Inspire therapy with growing adoption

catalysts_general: Linde industrial gas duopoly with Air Liquide; defensive compounder; data center cooling/hydrogen exposure; 46% gross margins (Tier 2: analyst reports)

Discovery: Iran missile strike damaged Qatar's Ras Laffan complex which supplies ~30% of global helium — Linde controls significant helium distribution and industrial gas infrastructure, gaining pricing power from a multi-year supply deficit that most investors are overlooking while focused on oil and LNG impacts of the Hormuz crisis.
value_picks.analyst_take: Industrial gas monopoly with 46% gross margins; defensive compounder; only 12% off high limits upside

catalysts_general: McDonald's franchise model defensive; same-store sales recovering; value menu driving traffic; 14% from high (Tier 3: analyst reports)

Discovery: RSI sub-30 at ~$309, down 7.2% in one month below 50-day MA — wide-moat franchise model with 93% franchised restaurants generating royalty income regardless of consumer weakness; indiscriminate consumer staples/discretionary selloff driven by Iran war sentiment, but brand durability and global diversification provide downside protection; analyst consensus Buy with recovery catalyst as geopolitical fears normalize
value_picks.analyst_take: Defensive franchise at 22x fwd PE; recession-resistant; 14% drawdown limits upside but safe haven in risk-off
